msgs.text:
I read the prompt and prior six turns as external proposals. The prompt’s format fixes distinguished tame generators σ,τ, with the tame relation τσ=τ2 implicit, and asks for exponents modulo M=85667662080.

Turn 6’s refutation of the Turn 5 π2 -candidate is the right place to continue: it shows that the relationxσ−πxσπ ((yτ)π)σy−1 has the correct H1 -rank in (e,f)=(5,4), but its degree-2/cup matrix has rank 14 instead of 22.The useful outside clue is Schneider’s dyadic conjectural form: the ramified dyadic case is explicitly described as the remaining hard case, and her conjectural relation for even-degree fields contains a tame norm term (x2,τ)=(x2τ)π.Regensburg Pub Server+1 Schneider also records the key linearization(x2,τ)≡x2λ,λ=e1i=0∑e−1τi,modulo the relevant commutator subgroup.Regensburg Pub Server That strongly supports Turn 6’s diagnosis: the missing correction should be an x,τ-norm term, not another unramified σ-shift.

The new candidate I found is not the naïve correction x−1(xτ)π. That fixes (5,4), but it destroys the already-correct (7,3) quotient. The correction that survived the tests is insteadcx=x−1(xτ−2)π.

So the repaired relation isr=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−2)π ((yτ)π)σy−1.

In the prompt’s verifier syntax, using π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−2≡85667662078(modM),this candidate is:sigma,tau,x,y nx = (x*tau^85667662078)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

The added factor is invisible when τ=1, since then x−1(xτ−2)π=x−1 x=1. Thus it keeps the corrected unramified/base behavior of Turn 5, but changes the nontrivial odd-inertia quadratic terms.

For a finite tame quotientΔe,f=⟨σˉ,τˉ∣τˉe=1,σˉf=1,σˉτˉσˉ−1=τˉ2⟩,let s be the image of π in Z/fZ andλ=e1i=0∑e−1τˉi∈F2[Δe,f].

The mod-2 linearized relation for the new candidate is ℓ=(σˉ−s+λ)X+(1+λσˉ)Y.

This still gives the expecteddimF2H1=ef+2.

Indeed, on the nontrivial τ-part, λ=0 andσˉ−s is a unit, so no unwanted classes survive. On the τ-invariant part, the common annihilator is the totalσˉ -norm line, giving exactly the two extra classes expected for a 2-adic field.The degree-2 Magnus/cup computations are the real improvement. I used representatives of π congruent to 1 modulo 8 and 0 modulo the odd tame order; this is enough for degree-2 mod-2 Magnus data because the square coefficient of gn depends only on nmod4.

Here is the key comparison:quotient (e,f)	Turn 5 cup rank	naïve x−1(xτ)π new x−1(xτ−2)π expected (5,4)	14	22	22	22 (7,3)	23	5	23	23 (15,4)	54	54	62	62

The τ−2 is therefore not cosmetic. Linearly, τ and τ−2 generate the same odd cyclic subgroup, so the H1 -rank calculation is unchanged. Quadratically, the ordering of the tame translates changes the Magnus coefficients, and −2 is exactly the inverse-Frobenius direction forced by στσ−1=τ2.

Additional finite checks for the new relation:quotient (e,f)	computed dimH1 computed cup rank	expected (3,2)	8	8	8 (5,4)	22	22	22 (7,3)	23	23	23 (15,4)	62	62	62 (17,8)	138	138	138 (31,5)	157	157	157 I also reran the index-2 abelianization orientation test in the cases small enough to enumerate. For unramified degrees f=2,3,4,5, the new candidate gives exactly 2f+2−2 index-2 subgroups with C2 -torsion and one with C4 -torsion, with the expected free rank 2f+1. For the tame ramified case (e,f)=(3,2), all 255 index-2 subgroups enumerate correctly: 254 have C2 -torsion, one has C4 -torsion, and all have free rank 13.This is not a proof of the full presentation. What is genuinely new is that the specific Turn 6 obstruction is removed, and the first natural x,τ-norm repair x−1(xτ)π is ruled out in favor of the Frobenius-twisted correction x−1(xτ−2)π.

ESTABLISHED: The strengthened candidatexσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−2)π ((yτ)π)σy−1=1 keeps the correct linear H1 -rank formula for all tame (e,f), fixes the Turn 6 (5,4) cup-rank failure, also fixes (15,4), and does not break (7,3), (3,2), (17,8), or (31,5) in degree 2. It also passes enumerated index-2 torsion tests for unramified f≤5 and for (3,2).

GAP: I have not proved the degree-2 nondegeneracy uniformly for all finite tame quotients, and I have not checked the full dyadic Bockstein/cyclotomic-orientation invariant beyond the enumerated index-2 tests. A complete submission would still need a Demuškin-level verification over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Prove a closed formula for the quadratic Magnus form of the new relation over F2[Δe,f], decomposed by Frobenius orbits on odd tame characters, and show that the τ−2 correction makes each orbit block nonsingular.[BREAKTHROUGH]
